evidence.title: Abdi Media report on NOTAM A1010/26 western Iran flight restrictions
evidence.source_description: Abdi Media article captured via web open/find on 2026-06-19; relevant page text lines 136-178 preserved as extract.
evidence.relevance: Corroborates NOTAM A1010/26 timing, western Tehran FIR airport closure, exceptions, authorization requirement, and security/military-control context through May 25, 2026.

evidence.title: OPSGROUP Middle East operational picture for Iran western restrictions
evidence.source_description: OPSGROUP operational article captured via web open/find on 2026-06-19; relevant page text lines 67-72, 111-131, 136-139 preserved as extract.
evidence.relevance: Independent aviation-operations context that western Iran remained heavily restricted/closed while eastern Tehran FIR had partial recovery-period reopening; helps distinguish foreign airline avoidance from operational airspace restrictions and supports that western Tehran FIR is a major regional airspace corridor.

evidence.title: Notamify display of OIIX A1010/26 raw NOTAM and interpretation
evidence.source_description: Notamify OIIX NOTAM page captured through web search/open on 2026-06-19. Page displays an interpretation and raw NOTAM text for A1010/26. Submitted for exact wording/scope nuance and provenance limits; Notamify is an aggregator, not the Iranian issuing authority.
evidence.relevance: Provides a raw NOTAM display indicating the restriction was framed as airport operations: all airports in the west part of Tehran FIR closed except listed airports operating sunrise-to-sunset, with new CAA permission required for civil IFR passenger flights. This helps assess whether plaintiff proved a broad airspace closure versus airport-operation restrictions, and highlights continued lack of official NOTAM custody.

evidence.title: Indian Express report on Iran western airspace NOTAM OIIX A1010/26
evidence.source_description: The Indian Express article captured via web open on 2026-06-19; relevant page text lines 18-24, 44-59, 100-112 preserved as extract.
evidence.relevance: Reports that Iran's Civil Aviation Authority issued NOTAM OIIX A1010/26 on May 22 closing western Tehran FIR airspace/airports until May 25, leaving only eight airports operational with daylight-only, fresh per-flight CAA approval; supports Iran-initiated, non-weather, timely, broad commercial aviation restriction.
